“DUFFLESS" by David M. Stern ACT ONE FADE IN: SCENE 1 INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY SCHOOL GYM ~ DAY

A banner reads "School Science Fair." SKINNER and KRABAPPEL survey the exhibits.

SKINNER Well Edna, for a school with no Asian kids, I think we put on a pretty darn good science fair.

They come to BART'S exhibit: a large pivot mounted ray gun. A sign reads "Go-go ray."

SKINNER (READING SIGN) Go-ga ray? BART Allow me to demonstrate.

Bart ZAPS Skinner with the ray gun. Skinner dances a frantic "Mashed Potato."

SKINNER Dah! What the...?

Krabappel LAUGHS. Bart ZAPS her with the ray. She starts to do the "Monkey." .

MRS. KRABAPPEL Can't... stop... doing... The Monkey!

Bart turns the ray on several OTHER PEOPLE causing them to do the "The Jerk," "The Swim," and " The Freddie."

BART

(LAUGHS)

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92

SKINNER (STILL DANCING) I'm disrupting the learning process... and I love it! (LAUGHS) Skinner dances over and pins a blue ribbon on Bart. SKINNER (CONT'D) First prize! DISSOLVE TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - BART'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS SKINNER (V.0.) First prize... first prize... LISA (V.0.) First prize... first prize... As Bart wakes up from his dream. We see Lisa shaking him. LISA First prize... first prize... BART Why are you saying that? LISA Just screwing with your mind. Lisa*x@IGGLES and exits. INT.x;SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN SCENE 2 MARGE (TO HOMER) Have you seen Lisa's Science

Fair Project?

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 :

HOMER (NOT LISTENING) Ahhh. Good ol' maple- flavored syrup.

LISA brings in a potted tomato plant. It bears a single tomato the size of a pumpkin.

LISA

I've grown a futuristic tomato by

fertilizing it with Anabolic steroids. BART

(SINCERE) The kind that help our Olympic

athletes reach new peaks of excellence? LISA

The very same. I think this tomato could

wipe out world hunger.

LISA'S FANTASY

A small THIRD WORLD FAMILY sits around the dinner table. The father carves Lisa's tomato like a turkey.

THIRD WORLD FATHER

More tomato? THIRD WORLD FAMILY

Yes please!

A portrait of Lisa hangs on the wall, smiling down at the ThirdiWorld family.

DISSOLVE TO MATCHING SHOT Lisa smiling at the table. Bart enters. HOMER

Where's your Science Fair project, boy?

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92

BART I thought I'd investigate the effects of cigarette smoking on dogs. SANTA'S LITTLE HELPER walks in smoking a cigarette. SANTA'S LITTLE HELPER (SMOKER'S COUGH) MARGE Bart, don't give the dog cigarettes! She yanks the cigarette out of the dog's mouth. tches and YAWNS. HOMER Whelp, time to go to work. HOMER'S BRAIN (V.0) Little do they know, I'm ducking out early to take the Duff Brewery tour. HOMER Roll in at nine, punch out at five. That's the plan. HOMER'S BRAIN (V.0) (SMUGLY) Heh, heh, heh, they don't suspect a thing. (THEN, BRIGHTLY) Well, off to the plant. HOMER (CRAFTILY) Then to the Duff Brewery. HOMER'S BRAIN (V.0)

Uh-oh. Did I say that or just think it?

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92

HOMER (PANICKY) I gotta think of a lie, fast. MARGE (CONFUSED) Homer, are you going to the Duff Brewery? HOMER (SCREAMS) Homer runs out. Marge, Bart and Lisa look at each other. INT. NUCLEAR POWER PLANT - DAY SC: 3

The overhead clock STRIKES twelve and a hideous horn BLARES in rapid sharp bursts.

LENNY (TO HOMER) Lunch time! SMITHERS comes over the P.A. speakers. SMITHERS (V.0.) Proceed directly to the cafeteria. Stragglers will be severely punished. The WORKERS head down the hallway like cattle. One stops to tie his shoe. He is roughly prodded back into line by a THUGGISH GUARD. Homer pulls out an ancient looking map. HOMER (READING) If the plant ye wish to flee/ Go to sector 7-B. Homer:opens a door labeled "7-B. DO NOT ENTER." He runs through a cobweb-filled hall past a skeleton in a hard hat. Finally he comes face to face with a seven-foot tall spider. HOMER (READING MAP) To overcome the spider's curse/ Simply quote a bible verse.

(THINKING) Thou shall not... uh...

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 |

Homer picks up a rock and throws it at the spider's head, knocking it unconscious. He runs past it to an open window and dives out. EXT. NUCLEAR POWER PLANT PARKING LOT = CONTINUOUS BARNEY'S car is idling with a mattress strapped to the roof. BARNEY (IN CAR, TO SELF) Hey, that looks like

Richard Simmons!

He pulls the car forward. Homer crashes to the ground where Barney's car had been.

BARNEY (CONT'D) Ah, wait. It's just a pile of rags. EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - DAY BCENE 4

The bell RINGS. CHILDREN race out the exits. Bart and Lisa exit together. Lisa is carrying her tomato.

LISA Oh Bart, I forgot my math book. (RE: TOMATO) Would you hold this for me, please?

BART (LIKE CURLY HOWARD) Soitenly.

Lisa runs back into school. A HUMMING Principal Skinner walks in front. of: Bart.

SKINNER Whoop. Shoe's untied. He leans over to tie it. SKINNER (CONT'D) Over, .under, in and out, that's what shoe

tying's all about.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 ©

Skinner's ass is now directly in front of Bart's line of fire. Bart looks at the tomato.

We push in on Skinner's butt. It wiggles. MUSIC: A TANTALIZING SIREN'S SONG. CLOSE UP TOMATO RACK FOCUS on Bart's tortured face in the background. He tosses the tomato. Skinner sees the shadow of the approaching tomato. He looks over his shoulder to see it coming right at him. SKINNEK (COVERING HIS EYES) No! NOT AGAIN! SKINNER'S FLASHBACK - 1969 - VIET NAM Skinner walking point with JOHNNY REBO. JOHNNY REBO (TEENAGED VOICE) When I get outta here I'm going straight to a bordello., SGT. SKINNER And I'll be with my mistress, grammar school administration. Suddenly, a barrage of sniper FIRE erupts. Skinner starts to run, but trips and falls. He looks up to see a Viet Cong soldier looming over him, holding a giant tomato. Skinner covers his eyes as the soldier raises the tomato over his head. SGT. SKINNER (CONT'D) (SCREAMS) END SKINNER FLASHBACK

Skinner continues to SCREAM as Lisa's tomato splatters all over hin.

SKINNER

(YELLS) Medic! Medic!

9F14 TAPE 8/13/92

Kids LAUGH at him. Lisa reemerges from school carrying her math book. She sees Skinner lying next to her ruined tomato.

LISA

BART But, Lisa, I brought laughter to the children. We hear a raucous burst of ugly LAUGHTER. BART (CONT'D) That's the sweetest music of all. EXT. DUFF BREWERY - DAY SCENE 5 Homer and Barney stand among the other TOURISTS looking at the Duff Brewery. A giant clock atop the Brewery reads three o'clock. A mechanical FIGURE comes out of the clock: it's a fat man in a t-shirt in a recliner holding a Duff. MECHANICAL FIGURE (THREE MECHANICAL BELCHES) The figure goes back into the clock. The smiling TOUR GUIDE approaches. TOUR GUIDE Welcome to the Duff Brewery. Has anyone taken our tour before? BARNEY (WAVING HAPPILY) Hi, Bill! I thought Jack did Wednesday afternoon. TOUR GUIDE (CHUCKLES) No, he switched so he could see

his grandson.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92

BARNEY Jeremy?

TOUR GUIDE That's right, Mr. Gumbel. (THEN) Now I'm sure you've all heard the rumors that a batch of Duff was contaminated with Strychnine.

The tourists AD-LIB "Strychnine?" "News to me." "First I've heard of it."

TOUR GUIDE (CONT'D) Are you sure? Everyone's talking about it. It was even on CNN last night. Everyone AD-LIBS "CNN," "Wow," "No we didn't." TOUR GUIDE (CONT'D) (QUICKLY) Well it's not true. Let's go. INT. DUFF MUSEUM - DAY : Duff paraphernalia is on display. We see a sign reading: "Prohibition Got You Down? Drink Doctor Duff's 'Health Tonic'." Next to that is a Cold War era poster that reads; "I Knew he was a Commie ‘Cause He Didn't Drink Duff Beer,"

showing a man drinking a Duff while a spy is hauled away. The tour group is watching an old Duff commercial on a monitor.

ON THE MONITOR ' We see the end of a 50's-style commercial of a crudely ANIMATED DOCTOR standing next to a schematic diagram of the human body. CARTOON DOCTOR Only Duff fills your q-zone (POINTS) with pure beer goodness.

The word "goodness" appears in PULSATING letters over the stomach area and floats up to the chest.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 1(

CARTOON DOCTOR (CONT'D) So drink up. And up! And up!

The doctor GLUGS down a bottle of Duff. He gets a goofy look and BLASTS off like a rocket.

TOUR GUIDE (PROUD) We're proud of all our Duff commercials. But here's a very special one from 1960. ON SCREEN SCENE 6

The NIXON-KENNEDY debate. Nixon answers a question, looking beady-eyed and unshaven.

NIXON Well I would suggest Mr. Vanocur, that if you knew the president, that that was probably a facetious remark.. . ANNOUNCERG And now a word from our sponsor.

Nixon and Kennedy step down from their podiums and meet halfway between.

KENNEDY I would like to take this opportunity to express my fondness for Duff beer. The audience CHEERS. NIXON I'd also like to express my fondness for that .particular beer.

The audience GRUMBLES.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92

HOMER (HATEFUL) The man never drank a Duff in his life. EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - PARKING LOT = DAY GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE is cleaning the tomato off the sidewalk. GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE Ach. He scoops a handful of tomato pulp into a trash can. LISA No wait, I can piece it back together! GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE Let it go, lass. It's no world for a tomato that big. INT. DUFF BREWERY - DAY TOUR GUIDE --. And here we have Duff, Duff Lite, and our newest flavor, Duff Dry.

We see three different vats. WIDEN TO see all three are being filled from the same spigot.

TOUR GUIDE What does the future hold for Duff? (COCKY) Let's just say we've got a few ideas up our sleeve.

HOMER Like what?

TOUR GUIDE

I'd rather not get into it right now.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 1

HOMER Why not? TOUR GUIDE (TESTY) All right, we don't have any ideas for the future. We got nothing. Happy? HOMER No.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN SCENE 7 Lisa sits at the table crying. Marge comforts her. LISA

Mom, Bart wrecked my project and the science fair's in three days! MARGE (DISPLEASED MURMUR) Well, I got an idea! Why don't you run a hamster through a maze? In a thought balloon, Lisa pictures a hamster running through a maze. Slowly the hamster's head turns into Bart's head. A gleam comes into Lisa's eyes BART IN MAZE (TINY VOICE) Help me! Help me! Lisazcomes out of her reverie. LISA (WICKED CHUCKLE) MARGE

What's so funny?

LISA Oh... I was just thinking of a joke I saw on "Herman's Head." (GIGGLES) Marge gives her a suspicious look. MARGE (DUBIOUS MURMUR) INT. DUFF BREWERY The tour group looks at an assembly line. TOUR GUIDE Now this is the most important man on the tour. He's in charge of quality control. ANGLE ON MAN As bottles go by, he pulls foreign objects out of them. MAN Fine. Fine. Mouse. Fine. Mouse. Rat. Fine. Finger. Fine. Syringe. Fine. BARNEY Lemme just say, you're doin' a great job, Phil. MAN (LOOKING UP) Hey, thanks a lot. That makes it all worthwhile. MAN

As he looks up we see beers go by with false teeth in them, a jagged broken bottle, and a swimming fish.

MAN

Fine... fine... nose... fine...

INT. DUFF BREWERY - TASTING ROOM - DAY SCENE 8

We see a dozen different kegs of Duff beer mounted on the wall. An inebriated Barney has his mouth on one of the taps.

HOMER (HOLDING BEER) Hey, Barney. I think you've had enough. BARNEY Are you crazy? We still haven't tried Raspberry Duff, Lady Duff, Tartar Control Duff... (PASSES OUT) Barney abruptly falls on his face. INT. DUFF BREWERY - PARKING LOT - DUSK We see a sign reading: "Friends Don't Let Friends Drive 2 Drunk." The other side flips over in strips. It now shows two friends sitting on the hood of a car toasting each other

with beer. The caption reads: "It's always time for Duff." Barney fumbles for his car keys.

HOMER Barney, give me your keys. You're too drunk to drive. BARNEY I'm fine. HOMER Okay, you leave me no option.

Homer SLUGS Barney in the jaw. Barney staggers but doesn't go down.

BARNEY Ow. What was that for? HOMER

I'm trying to knock you out.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 1

Homer grabs a tire iron and HITS Barney over the head with it. BARNEY Ow, cut it out.

Homer, pulling Barney by the hair, opens the car door and BLAMS Barney's head in it several times.

BARNEY (CONT'D) Ow! Ow! Ow! Awright, here, take the keys! Barney gives Homer the keys, crawls in the passenger side and passes out. Homer gets in the driver's seat. PAN over to the Duff mascot, a giant smiling beer tankard. The mascot waves goodbye, then lifts its lid revealing CHIEF WIGGUM inside. WIGGUM (INTO WALKIE-TALKIE) Look alive, boys. Coupla stewed prunes headed your way. OUTSIDE THE ENTRANCE - DUSK SCENE 9 Behind a billboard reading: "Now Leaving Duff Country," we see MOTORCYCLE COPS lined up six deep. Homer pulls out of the parking lot. He hasn't gone more than ten feet when he hears SIRENS. Lights start flashing. HOMER Gulp. DISSOLVE TO: EXT. DUFF BREWERY ENTRANCE - LATER

Homer stands on one foot touching his nose with his eyes closed

HOMER (SINGING) W,X,Y, and Z/ Now I know ny ABC's/ Won't you come and play with me? EDDIE

Flawless.

LOU We also would have accepted "Tell me what you think of me."

EDDIE I guess you're free to...

Barney sticks his head out of the passenger window. He's no longer so drunk.

BARNEY Give him a breathalizer!

HOMER Shut up, Barney!

Eddie gives him a breathalizer.

EDDIE Forget it. We're not getting a reading. BARNEY Did you remember to press the reset button? EDDIE Gee, thanks. (TO HOMER) You're under arrest.

As Homer is handcuffed, Wiggum approaches in his tankard costume.

WIGGUM (TO BARNEY) You think you can get this car home?

BARNEY

Sure thing, Giant Beer.

SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN Marge answers the phone. MARGE Hello. INTERCUT With Wiggum at the police station. WIGGUM Ah, yeah, Mrs. Simpson, I have some bad news. Your husband was found DOA. My name is Mrs. Phillips. You said my

husband is DWI.

strategy to get you out of here. (HUSHED

him.

JUDGE Your license is hereby revoked and you are to attend traffic school and two months of Alch-anon meetings. And as a side note, I recommend that you drop thirty to forty pounds. HOMER I'll start dieting tomorrow. JUDGE You're under oath, Mr. Simpson. HOMER I'm picturing you as a large ham right now. INT. DMV - DAY Patty STAMPS "VOID" on Homer's driver's license. PATTY It doesn't get any better than this. INT. “ALL CREATURES GREAT AND CHEAP" PET SHOP - DAY Lisa stands at the counter. LISA I want the most intelligent hamster you've got.

The pet store OWNER reaches under the counter into a box of hamsters and pulls one out at random.

WISE GUY PET SHOP OWNER Okayyy... This little guy writes mysteries

under the name of J. D. MacGregor.

LISA How can a hamster write mysteries?

WISE GUY PET SHOP OWNER Well, he gets the ending first, then he works backward.

LISA Oh, come on.

WISE GUY PET SHOP OWNER Look kid, just take him before his mother eats him. All right?

INT. MARGE'S CAR - DAY SCENE 12

Marge drives. Homer "back seat drives" from the passenger seat.

HOMER Pass that guy... run that light... cut across that lawn... give that guy the bird... MARGE No! HOMER All right then, moon hin. MARGE I am not mooning an ambulance. HOMER (DISAPPOINTED) You've changed, Marge. EXT. SIMPSON DRIVEWAY - MORNING

Marge waves goodbye.

MARGE Have a good day at work, honey. Homer pulls out of the driveway on a girl's bike. His lunch pail is in the basket. NELSON rides past Homer and sneers at this bike. NELSON Haw haw! HOMER (MUTTERING) Stupid bicycle. Can't believe this. No good, rotten... Homer RINGS the bell and LAUGHS. HOMER (CONT'D) I guess it's not all bad. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LISA'S BEDROOM = DAY SCENE 13 Lisa writes in her lab notebook. LISA I propose to determine the answer to the question: "Is my brother dumber than a hamster?" Bart walks by. BART Hey Lis, look what I can do. Bart sticks his entire fist in his mouth. It won't come out. BART (CONT'D) (MUFFLED) Doggone it.

Lisa and the hamster look at each other.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LISA'S DOLL HOUSE ~ DAY The hamster sits in the Malibu Stacey living room. A card reads "Experiment One." A food pellet sits on top of a miniature bookshelf. After careful consideration, the hamster pushes a miniature couch to the bookshelf, hops up on it and grabs the pellet. LISA (GASPS) Remarkable. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE ~ LIVING ROOM - DAY Bart enters the living room -- it is set up like the doll house. His fist is now out of his mouth. He sees a cupcake on the bookshelf. BART Stupid books. (NOTICING) Hey, a cupcake. He walks over, tries jumping at it with no luck. He starts to climb the bookshelf. It CRASHES to the ground pinning Bart underneath. BART (CONT'D) Yaaah! Maggie crawls by and makes off with the cupcake. Lisa observes and shakes her head. She writes on her notebook "Hamster-1; Bart-0." LISA (V.0.) Hamster one... Bart... zero. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LISA'S BEDROOM - LATER

In the hanster cage, a food pellet is connected to electrical wires and a small battery.

LISA (V.0) Experiment two: The food is connected to a mild.electrical current.

The hamster walks up to the pellet, tries to eat it and gets a mild shock. The hamster looks startled.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 2

LISA (CONT'D) (WRITING) The hanster looks puzzled.

She looks back into the cage and the hamster leaps out at her, hissing and snapping its teeth. Lisa pulls back.

LISA (CONT'D) (WRITING, SHAKEN) ... And somewhat peeved. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN ~- DAY Bart enters the kitchen WHISTLING a merry tune. He spots a cupcake marked "Do Not Touch" on the counter. He grabs it and is shocked. BART (LIKE CURLY HOWARD) Oooh - wise guy, eh? He grabs for the cupcake and is shocked several times. BART Ow... OW... Ow!

His hair sticks out crazily. Lisa watches from the doorway. She writes in her notebook: "Hamster-2; Bart-0."lisa

(GIGGLES) Hampster two... Bart zero...

INT. DRIVER TRAINING SCHOOL - NIGHT SCENE 14

Homer sits in a classroom with other traffic offenders. Chief Wiggum lectures in front of a screen.

WIGGUM Now if any of you ever think of drinking and driving again, this film will scare the pants off of you. On screen comes a home movie of Wiggum in his bathing suit

WIGGUM Guys, guys, this is the wrong movie. (BEAT) Oh, wait a second, I do something really funny here.

on screen MRS. WIGGUM comes out in a dress, with a platter of food. Wiggum splashes her with a garden hose. She looks very

unhappy. WIGGUM (CONT'D) (LAUGH WHICH FADES) BACK TO SCENE Eddie switches movies on the projector. ON SCREEN a driver's educational film comes on, hosted by TROY MCCLURE. He stands on a freeway in front of a big traffic accident. TROY MCCLURE (SADLY) What a terrible waste. (CHIPPER) Hi! I'm actor, Troy McClure. You might remember me from such Driver's Ed films as "Alice's Adventures through the Windshield Glass" and "The Decapitation of Larry Leadfoot." For the next 60 minutes we'll be seeing actual film of car crash victims. ANGLE ON THE CLASS We sea their reaction to the film. MUSIC: WACKY COMEDY MUSIC TROY (V.O) Here's an appealing fellow. In fact they're "a-peeling" him off the sidewalk.

PAN across horrified faces to Homer CHUCKLING.

Homer stands before REVEREND LOVEJOY, who sits behind a card table.

HOMER I'm here for the Alch-anon meeting. REVEREND LOVEJOY Huh uh, third door on your left. Homer walks down the hall. JASPER walks up to Lovejoy. REVEREND LOVEJOY Coping with senility? JASPER (INDIGNANT) No! I'm here for microwave cookery. No, wait. Coping with senility. INT. CHURCH BASEMENT ROOM - NIGHT

Homer sits in the back of the room, looking at all of the PEOPLE assembled. NED FLANDERS stands up.

FLANDERS My name is Ned.

ASSEMBLY Hello Ned.

Flanders holds up a large chip.

9F14 FINAL DELIVERY 8/13/92 2

FLANDERS It's been four thousand days since my last drink. It was my first and last Blackberry schnapps. PLASHBACK: SCENE 16

Flanders is in his college fraternity drinking blackberry schnapps. He SINGS "Good Morning Starshine."

FLANDERS Saba suby shaba/ Nubie abba naba/ lay lay lo lo. BACK TO SCENE FLANDERS (SOBBING) I was more animal than man. OTTO stands up. OTTO My name is Ot-to. I love to get blot-to. MOLE MAN stands up. MOLE MAN My name is Hans. Drinking has ruined ny life. I'm thirty-one years old. SMITHERS stands up. SMITHERS I'm Waylon Smithers. I'm just here to get the names of all the alcoholics who work at the plant. ASSEMBLY

(PLEASANTLY) Hello Waylon.

HOMER AND MARGE'S BEDROOM - NIGHT BCENE 17 Marge is lying in bed reading a pamphlet titled, "IS YOUR SPOUSE A SOUSE?". Homer is in the bathroom, brushing his teeth. cakes for all.
